title: Data Standards for Humanoid Robotics: The Missing Infrastructure for Physical AI
note: arXiv:2606.19769. Primary source. $180 vs $2.20/hr cost ratio, 40ms→10m position error, 849ms→3.96 m/s velocity estimation error directly cited.

title: Open X-Embodiment: Robotic Learning Datasets and RT-X Models
container-title: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA 2024)
note: arXiv:2310.08864. 1M+ trajectories across 22 embodiments from 34 labs.

title: DROID: A Large-Scale In-The-Wild Robot Manipulation Dataset
All Robots in One (ARIO): A Comprehensive Unified Embodied Dataset
note: arXiv:2408.10899. Preprint — 3M+ multi-modal trajectories including 5 modalities.

title: On the Data Gap in Robot Learning
note: Source for '100,000-year gap': language model training data ≈ 100K human-years vs. largest robot teleoperation dataset ≈ 1 year. IEEE RAS Automation Research Spotlights presentation.

title: A Survey of Embodied Artificial Intelligence Data Engineering
note: Identifies three bottlenecks in embodied AI data: (1) high collection cost, (2) data silos, (3) evaluation void.

title: Humanoid robot datasets — Part 1: General requirements
note: Working Draft. Two-layer architecture: horizontal infrastructure (lifecycle, provenance, quality, versioning, traceability) + capability modules (manipulation, locomotion, HRI, cognition).

title: Data quality for analytics and machine learning (daml)
note: Lineage standard. ISO/WD 26264-1 extends this data quality framework to robotic datasets.

title: Humanoid Robot: The AI Accelerant
note: Forecast: ~250,000 units by 2030 (base case), 1.4M units / $38B by 2035.

title: Chinese Firms Dominated Global Humanoid Robot Shipments in 2025
note: ~13,000 humanoid robot units shipped globally in 2025; China accounted for 85–90% share.

title: Position Paper: Revolutionising the Robotics Ecosystem Through Enhanced Interoperability
note: UK government agency policy recommendation for open interoperability standards in robotics.

title: AgiBot World 2026 dataset open-source to accelerate embodied AI development
note: Secondary source on AgiBot World dataset: 1M+ trajectories, 2,976.4 hours, open-sourced 2026.

title: LeRobot Datasets — October 2025 Update
note: PyTorch-based de facto robot data format; one of four major fragmented formats alongside RLDS, HDF5, ARIO.
